comments.body: Can you provide a link to the "solution" you found when googling? I assume it uses a mouse listener and prevents the event.
Haven't tried, but you could try to add selection/click listeners and then do event.doit = false. That usually blocks events from happening.
event.doit = false does not work. My doubleclick handler is called later then the event for collapsing/expanding.

answers.body:
// Prevent double-click expand/collapse
tree.addListener(SWT.DefaultSelection, event -> {
    event.doit = false;
});

This prevents the default double-click behavior entirely. You can still expand/collapse with the arrow or single-click if needed.

If you want double-click to do something else (like open an item), add that logic after event.doit = false; or you can create/add more listeners.

You must remove this listener in order for it to stop preventing the event. I use a mouse listener for my double-click logic.

This will set the expand/collapse to what it was before clicking:

tree.addMouseListener(new MouseAdapter() {
    @Override
    public void mouseDoubleClick(MouseEvent e) {
    // if not using previous listener - user can see this happen
    TreeItem item = tree.getSelection()[0];
    item.setExpanded(!item.getExpanded()); // "undo" expand/collapse
    // your double-click logic.
    }
});

Use the default selection listener, then only your logic in a mouse listener.

title: Disable expand on tree after doubleclick
body:
I'm using SWT for our gui. We are using Tree to get a tree menu. Now we want following behavior: - When double clicked on the parent -> open screen, no expand/collapse of the tree - When using the arrow (on the left of the parent item) -> expand/collapse the tree

Currently when doubleclicking on the parent item will open the screen and expand the tree. After some Googling I found some solutions but when I implement those I can open the screen without expanding the tree. But cannot expand the tree in another way.

For opening the screen from the parent I use this handler:

operationMenu.addListener(SWT.MouseDoubleClick, new Listener() {

        @Override
        public void handleEvent(Event event) {
        }
};

How can we implement this in SWT?

Urls that I have use: SWT: prevent Tree from expanding by doubleclick?
